summary: Walk src/integration/rag/* and remove seams that don't earn keep. Phase 1 = seam inventory (discovery; non-skippable). Anchor case (chunk-converter.ts:60-63) requires ORCHESTRATOR ruling on Option A/B/C before its atomic removal commit lands. Phase 2+ = per-seam atomic removals, each gated by tsc + frozen-fixture invariant + AC-05a/b real-world identity preservation. Investigation-first by structure; per-seam disposition is the load-bearing decision.

anchor_case.issue: Silent skip with zero observable signal — no counter, no log, no chunksSkippedDetails entry, no failure surface
anchor_case.resolution_options: Three options A/B/C documented in context.scope.scope_decision_required block; ORCHESTRATOR rules

predecessor_seam_deletions_already_lande: WO-RAG-INDEX-SINGLE-ANALYZER-SLICE-001 deleted the facet enrichment block (chunk-converter.ts:462-564 at the time) and the second analyzerService.analyze call in indexing-orchestrator.ts. Two major seams already gone.
WO-CLEANUP-INDEXING-ORCHESTRATOR-ANALYZERSERVICE-PARAM-001 removed the @deprecated analyzerService constructor param + private field. Mechanical cleanup of the post-pivot-1 dependency surface.

safety_net_in_place.ci_blocking_confirmed: Mutation-guard Path A demonstrated CI exit 1 on identity-breaking input mutation
safety_net_in_place.test_assertions: AC-05a element-grain identity (chunksSkipped count === graph hs=missing nodes); AC-05b file-grain identity (uniqueFiles size === validation-report.header_missing_count); cross-check; distinctness
safety_net_in_place.limits: Frozen fixture has no phantom-import case; if a seam removal targets phantom-import behavior specifically, the invariant alone won't catch a regression — manual real-codebase static probe (predecessor verify-dual-ac.mjs) is the secondary guard

investigation_targets.rationale: The seam inventory cannot be enumerated at scoping time — that IS the work of Phase 1. SKILLS surfaced the four broad target areas from the stub (chunk-converter.ts, indexing-orchestrator.ts, broader sweep, __tests__/) but the per-file per-seam list with disposition is what Phase 1 produces.

implementation_strategy.approach: Phase 1 inventory (one commit: inventory.md authored). Anchor-case ruling block surfaced post-Phase-1 if not yet ruled. Phase 2+ atomic-commit removals (one seam per commit). Closeout phase ties up communication + LOC accounting.

phases_summary.label: Seam inventory + anchor-case ruling surface
phases_summary.deliverable: coderef/workorder/rag-index-seam-deletion/inventory.md enumerating every seam in src/integration/rag/* with: file, lines, current code, classification (silent_skip|swallowed_error|dead_branch|pass_through|justified_keep), proposed disposition (delete|promote|keep), rationale. The anchor case (chunk-converter.ts:60-63) is enumerated with all three options surfaced verbatim from context.json scope_decision_required block.
phases_summary.halt_condition: If Phase 1 surfaces ambiguity beyond the anchor case (e.g., a second seam with multiple legitimate paths), HALT and surface to ORCHESTRATOR for ruling before Phase 2.

phases_summary.label: Per-seam atomic removals (ruling-blocked seams skipped until ruled)
phases_summary.commits: 5-15 anticipated based on stub inventory targets; finalized post-Phase-1
phases_summary.deliverable: One atomic commit per seam removal. Commit message names the seam (file:lines), the disposition (delete|promote), and the rationale. Each commit's tsc + frozen-fixture invariant + AC-05a/b real-world checks are documented in the commit message.
phases_summary.halt_condition: Any commit that trips the frozen-fixture invariant OR breaks AC-05a/b real-world identity → HALT immediately. Any seam that turns out to genuinely earn keep on closer inspection (handles a real failure mode) → leave in place with explicit justification comment, document in inventory.md update.

phases_summary.label: Closeout-ready handoff
phases_summary.deliverable: communication.json status=complete with closeout-ready marker; final LOC accounting; final seam-disposition table; CODEREF-CORE → ORCHESTRATOR communication_log entry summarizing the commit chain + per-seam outcomes.

verification_per_commit: vitest run src/integration/rag/__tests__/ — frozen-fixture invariant + all rag-suite tests PASS
vitest run __tests__/pipeline/ — Phase 7 chokepoint INVARIANT + AC-05 dual-identity (frozen-fixture-loaded) PASS
Optional manual: rerun predecessor WO static probe verify-dual-ac.mjs against current real codebase; AC-05a 2415===2415 + AC-05b 263===263 still PASS

risks.description: Phase 1 inventory misses a seam; that seam ships unaudited
risks.mitigation: Inventory uses grep for explicit patterns (try { ... } catch, ?? null, || {}, async (...)=>{...catch{}}, fileExists guards) plus eyeball read of every file in src/integration/rag/*. Document the search method in inventory.md so future audits can re-run it.

risks.description: A seam removal in Phase 2 silently breaks production behavior on a real-codebase edge case the frozen-fixture doesn't exercise
risks.impact: regression in real coderef-core rag-index runs (e.g., on a project with phantom imports or unusual file shapes)
risks.mitigation: Each Phase 2 commit re-runs the predecessor WO static probe (verify-dual-ac.mjs) against the current real codebase as part of the verification gate, in addition to the frozen-fixture invariant. If real-world AC-05a/b identity shifts post-removal, HALT immediately.

risks.description: Anchor case Option A is ruled but DR-PHASE-7-B (locked-additive IndexingResult schema) is hard, blocking Option A
risks.impact: anchor case removal blocked; Options B or C are the fallback
risks.mitigation: Option A's blast radius includes a SkipReason enum addition. If ORCHESTRATOR rules DR-PHASE-7-B is hard, Options B (delete) or C (log-only) are still available. The scope_decision_required block flags this conditional dependency.

risks.description: Inventory turns up a 'seam' that's actually load-bearing — a fallback branch or pass-through that is needed for a documented edge case the WO author didn't realize
risks.impact: false-positive; the seam should NOT be removed
risks.mitigation: Per-seam disposition includes a 'keep-with-justification' option. Any seam that turns out to handle a real failure mode gets 'keep' with a single-sentence comment naming the failure mode (per stub earning-keep criterion #3). LOC reduction target accommodates this; not every inventoried seam is required to be removed.

risks.description: Test updates in Phase 2 accidentally weaken the substrate-identity assertions
risks.impact: frozen-fixture invariant or AC-05 dual-identity test loses bite
risks.mitigation: Frozen-fixture file contents are out-of-scope by directive — they cannot be modified. Tests in src/integration/rag/__tests__/* OTHER THAN the frozen-fixture loader can be modified, but only to adjust expectations (silent-skip → explicit-failure assertion). Boundary: do NOT change what the test asserts about substrate identity.

expected_outcome.loc_delta: Net negative target. Stub estimate 5-15 seams; if average -5 LOC per removal = -25 to -75 net. Some removals may be larger (delete entire pass-through helper class). Final number depends on Phase 1 inventory.
expected_outcome.files_changed: Likely 5-10 files in src/integration/rag/* + corresponding test updates
expected_outcome.test_pass_rate: 100% — frozen-fixture invariant + AC-05 dual-identity + Phase 7 chokepoint INVARIANT + all other rag-suite tests preserved
expected_outcome.real_world_substrate_identity: AC-05a 2415===2415 + AC-05b 263===263 preserved (verified per-commit via static probe)
